frame_analyses.description: Initial view of EMIG laparoscopic trainer with Penrose drain positioned at 45-degree angle on white foam pad
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup appears standard with drain properly positioned for suturing

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver visible entering field with suture material
Needle driver grasping suture near Penrose drain, positioning for first bite
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument positioning, preparing to engage tissue

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through drain tissue, creating first pass
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle passage through Penrose material

frame_analyses.description: Completing suture placement with both ends of suture visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture tails appear adequate length for knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw construction with instruments manipulating suture tails
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be creating surgeon's knot with double wrap

frame_analyses.description: Working on second throw, opposite direction from first
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper alternating direction for square knot construction

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed suture with knot tied and Penrose drain approximated
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure, drain edges well approximated

knot_assessments.notes: Double wrap first throw consistent with surgeon's knot technique
Single throw in opposite direction, proper square knot progression

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent tissue approximation with no visible gaps, secure closure achieved

technique_summary: Efficient intracorporeal suturing with proper surgeon's knot followed by square knot completion. Good tissue approximation achieved with minimal wasted motion.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly faster instrument movements to improve time efficiency
Maintain consistent tension throughout knot tying sequence
strengths: Excellent tissue approximation with complete closure
Proper surgeon's knot technique with alternating throws
Clean needle passage and suture placement
